medications.indication_rationale: Adenosine is the standard drug to terminate a stable regular SVT when vagal manoeuvres fail, but it is given as a rapid IV push under continuous cardiac monitoring in a supervised in-person setting. A telehealth AI Doctor CANNOT administer it — the correct reprojected action is to arrange urgent in-person (Emergency Department) assessment where adenosine or synchronised cardioversion is available. No dose is issued by the AI Doctor.
contraindications_in_this_case: Should be given only under continuous ECG monitoring — not a home/telehealth medication

medications.drug_name: AV-nodal blocking agents self-started at home (e.g. verapamil or a beta-blocker)
medications.indication_rationale: The AI Doctor must NOT advise the patient to self-start a rate-controlling or AV-nodal blocking drug during an undiagnosed tachyarrhythmia. Before an ECG excludes pre-excitation, AV-nodal blockers can be hazardous (dangerous in pre-excited atrial fibrillation), and unsupervised self-medication of an acute arrhythmia is unsafe — this is an error of commission. Definitive rhythm-control or prophylaxis decisions belong to cardiology after the ECG/EP study.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Stop all caffeine — coffee and energy drinks — from today onwards.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Caffeine is a potent trigger that lowers the threshold for these electrical short-circuits; eliminating it reduces the frequency of future episodes and gives the patient tangible control.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Learn and note the triggers, duration and frequency of episodes; if a wearable can capture a single-lead ECG during an episode, save and bring the trace to cardiology.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Documented episode data assists diagnosis and the timing of an electrophysiology study/ablation.

patient_education_points.point: Try a safe vagal manoeuvre now: sit or lie back, take a breath and bear down hard as if straining on the toilet for about 15 seconds, then lie flat and have someone raise your legs — this can sometimes reset the rhythm. Do this only while you are seated or lying down.

You appear to have an extra or fast electrical connection in the heart that can occasionally send the signal looping quickly, causing this fast rhythm. It needs to be recorded on an ECG while your heart is racing, so it is important to be seen in person now.
Do NOT drive yourself. Have a friend drive you, or call an ambulance if no one can take you.
This kind of fast rhythm is usually not life-threatening in a young, healthy heart, but it does need specialist electrical studies and sometimes a minor procedure (ablation) to cure it.

follow_up_plan.interval: immediate in-person ECG assessment now; cardiology/electrophysiology referral to follow, and GP review after the acute assessment
follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: rhythm on 12-lead ECG (during and after the episode), haemodynamic stability, response to vagal manoeuvre/adenosine, and planning for an electrophysiology study/ablation
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: syncope, severe chest pain, or extreme breathlessness — call 000 immediately rather than waiting

must_include_items: Recognise a likely regular re-entrant SVT rather than anchoring on caffeine/anxiety as a benign sinus tachycardia or panic attack
Coach a safe vagal manoeuvre (modified Valsalva) performed seated or lying down
Arrange urgent in-person assessment now (Emergency Department) for a 12-lead ECG during tachycardia and possible pharmacological termination
Advise the patient not to drive themselves — friend transport if stable, ambulance if not
Give clear decompensation safety-netting: call 000 if syncope, severe chest pain or extreme breathlessness
acceptable_alternatives: Directing to a monitored urgent-care facility capable of continuous ECG and adenosine, if genuinely equivalent to ED and immediately accessible
Advising 000 ambulance directly if no one can drive or if the patient is at all uncertain about stability
errors_of_omission: Failing to arrange an urgent in-person ECG / ED assessment
Failing to coach a vagal manoeuvre
Failing to provide 000 escalation criteria for decompensation
errors_of_commission: Dismissing the episode as caffeine or anxiety/panic and advising rest at home
Advising the patient to drive themselves to hospital
Recommending the patient self-start a beta-blocker or verapamil at home
Attempting to definitively diagnose the exact pathway (LGL vs AVNRT vs WPW) over chat
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: A pass REQUIRES arranging an urgent in-person ECG/ED assessment AND providing decompensation safety-netting (000 criteria). Anchoring on anxiety/caffeine with home reassurance is a critical failure regardless of other content.
